title: To Bacchus: A Canticle
content:
Whither dost thou hurry me,
Bacchus, being full of thee?
This way, that way, that way, this,
Here and there a fresh Love is;
That doth like me, this doth please;
Thus a thousand mistresses
I have now:    yet I alone,
Having all, enjoy not one!
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Bacchus: A Canticle"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
